content:
The user wants me to extract the table from the provided image. **1. Identify the table structure:** The image contains a table titled "Table 4 Test condition, procedure and requirements". The table has four main columns: - TEST - TEST METHOD - PROCEDURE - REQUIREMENTS **2. Analyze the rows:** - **Row 1 (Header):** TEST, TEST METHOD, PROCEDURE, REQUIREMENTS - **Row 2:** Temperature Coefficient of Resistance (T.C.R.) - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 304; JIS C 5202-4.8 - Procedure: At +25/-55 °C and +25/+125 °C - Formula: $T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C) - Where: - $t_1 = +25$ °C or specified room temperature - $t_2 = -55$ °C or +125 °C test temperature - $R_1$ = resistance at reference temperature in ohms - $R_2$ = resistance at test temperature in ohms - Requirements: Refer to table 3 - **Row 3:** Thermal Shock - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 107G; IEC 60115-1 4.19 - Procedure: At -65 (+0/-10) °C for 2 minutes and at +155 (+10/-0) °C for 2 minutes; 25 cycles - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. - **Row 4:** Low Temperature Operation - Test Method: MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.4 - Procedure: At -65 (+0/-5) °C for 1 hour; RCWV applied for 45 (+5/-0) minutes - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage - **Row 5:** Short Time Overload - Test Method: MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.5; IEC 60115-1 4.13 - Procedure: $2.5 \times$ RCWV applied for 5 seconds at room temperature - Requirements: ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage - **Row 6:** Insulation Resistance - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 302; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 - Procedure: RCOV for 1 minute - Type: RC0805 - Voltage (DC): 300 V - Requirements: ≥10 GΩ - **Row 7:** Dielectric Withstand Voltage - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 301; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 - Procedure: Maximum voltage ($V_{rms}$) applied for 1 minute - Type: RC0805 - Voltage (AC): 300 $V_{rms}$ - Requirements: No breakdown or flashover - **Row 8:** Resistance to Soldering Heat - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 210C; IEC 60115-1 4.18 - Procedure: Unmounted chips; 260 ±5 °C for 10 ±1 seconds - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage - **Row 9:** Life - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 108A; IEC 60115-1 4.25.1 - Procedure: At 70±2 °C for 1,000 hours; RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off - Requirements: ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. **3. Format the output:** I will format this as a Markdown table. I need to be careful with the nested table structure for "Insulation Resistance" and "Dielectric Withstand Voltage". **4. Refine the content:** - Ensure all units are correct. - Ensure formulas are represented correctly. - Ensure the "Where" section is clear. Let's construct the markdown. ```markdown | TEST | TEST METHOD | PROCEDURE | REQUIREMENTS | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Temperature Coefficient of Resistance (T.C.R.) | MIL-STD-202F-method 304; JIS C 5202-4.8 | At +25/-55 °C and +25/+125 °C
Formula:
$T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C)
Where
$t_1 = +25$ °C or specified room temperature
$t_2 = -55$ °C or +125 °C test temperature
$R_1$ = resistance at reference temperature in ohms
$R_2$ = resistance at test temperature in ohms | Refer to table 3 | | Thermal Shock | MIL-STD-202F-method 107G; IEC 60115-1 4.19 | At -65 (+0/-10) °C for 2 minutes and at +155 (+10/-0) °C for 2 minutes; 25 cycles | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | | Low Temperature Operation | MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.4 | At -65 (+0/-5) °C for 1 hour; RCWV applied for 45 (+5/-0) minutes | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Short Time Overload | MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.5; IEC 60115-1 4.13 | $2.5 \times$ RCWV applied for 5 seconds at room temperature | ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Insulation Resistance | MIL-STD-202F-method 302; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 | RCOV for 1 minute
Type RC0805
Voltage (DC) 300 V | ≥10 GΩ | | Dielectric Withstand Voltage | MIL-STD-202F-method 301; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 | Maximum voltage ($V_{rms}$) applied for 1 minute
Type RC0805
Voltage (AC) 300 $V_{rms}$ | No breakdown or flashover | | Resistance to Soldering Heat | MIL-STD-202F-method 210C; IEC 60115-1 4.18 | Unmounted chips; 260 ±5 °C for 10 ±1 seconds | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Life | MIL-STD-202F-method 108A; IEC 60115-1 4.25.1 | At 70±2 °C for 1,000 hours; RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off | ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | ``` I will double check the "Insulation Resistance" and "Dielectric Withstand Voltage" sections. They have sub-rows for Type and Voltage. I will represent them as nested tables or just text within the cell to maintain the structure. A nested table is cleaner. For "Insulation Resistance": | Type | RC0805 | | --- | --- | | Voltage (DC) | 300 V | For "Dielectric Withstand Voltage": | Type | RC0805 | | --- | --- | | Voltage (AC) | 300 $V_{rms}$ | This looks good. I will proceed with generating the response. One detail: "Maximun voltage" is a typo in the original image, I should probably keep it as is or correct it. I'll keep it as is to be faithful to the source. Also, the "Where" section in the first row is quite long. I'll format it nicely. Final check on the image content: - "Product specification" is at the top right. - "7/10" is in a box. - "YAGEO" logo is top left. - "Chip Resistor Surface Mount" is the title. - "RC SERIES 0805 (Pb Free)" is below the title. - "TESTS AND REQUIREMENTS" is the section title. - "Table 4 Test condition, procedure and requirements" is the table caption. The table extraction seems complete. I will now generate the markdown. One small detail: The "Where" section lists variables. I will use text for clarity. $t_1=+25$ °C or specified room temperature $t_2=-55$ °C or +125 °C test temperature $R_1$=resistance at reference temperature in ohms $R_2$=resistance at test temperature in ohms The formula is $T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C) Okay, ready to write. One final check on the "Life" row requirements: ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. Correct. The "Resistance to Soldering Heat" requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage Correct. The "Dielectric Withstand Voltage" procedure: Maximun voltage ($V_{rms}$) applied for 1 minute Type RC0805 Voltage (AC) 300 $V_{rms}$ Correct. The "Insulation Resistance" procedure: RCOV for 1 minute Type RC0805 Voltage (DC) 300 V Correct. The "Short Time Overload" requirements: ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage Correct. The "Low Temperature Operation" requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage Correct. The "Thermal Shock" requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. Correct. The "Temperature Coefficient of Resistance" requirements: Refer to table 3 Correct. Everything seems in order. I will produce the markdown now. I will use standard markdown table syntax. For the nested tables, I will use standard markdown table syntax inside the cell. For the formula, I will use LaTeX syntax. For the "Where" section, I will use a list or just text. A list is better. One detail: "Maximun" is indeed a typo in the source, I will keep it. Another detail: "RCWV" is used multiple times. Another detail: "GΩ" is used for Gigaohms. Okay, let's go. The footer has "Sep 03, 2004 V.2" and "www.yageo.com". I will include these if relevant, but the main request is the table. I'll focus on the table. The user prompt is just the image, so I should provide the content of the image. The main content is the table. I will start with the table. I will also include the title and caption. I will not include the header/footer unless it's part of the table structure, which it isn't really. Wait, the "Product specification" and "7/10" are part of the document header, not the table. The "YAGEO" logo is also header. The "Chip Resistor Surface Mount RC SERIES 0805 (Pb Free)" is a subheader. The "TESTS AND REQUIREMENTS" is a section title. The "Table 4..." is the table caption. So I will start with the table. One last check on the "Insulation Resistance" row. The "Procedure" column has: RCOV for 1 minute Type RC0805 Voltage (DC) 300 V This is effectively a small table. Same for "Dielectric Withstand Voltage". I will format these as nested tables. Okay, plan is solid. I will write the markdown now.
